channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bluesky photo-sharing app Flashes launches in beta
item.description: Flashes, a photo-sharing app that’s linked to X-alternative Bluesky, launched in beta this week before disappearing from the test store due to what its developer said was technical difficulties.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Telescopes spy a monster radio jet streaming from a bright and early object in the universe
item.description: Telescopes around the world have spotted a monster radio jet streaming from a quasar dating back to the first 1 billion years of the universe.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House lawmakers push to ban AI app DeepSeek from US government devices
item.description: Lawmakers in the House are proposing to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from U.S. government devices.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Invincible season 3 is already laying the groundwork for one of the graphic novel series' bloodiest battles in an unusual way
item.description: Invincible season 3 is already teasing its next big and brutal event – and you might not have noticed how it's doing so.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Why I want glasses that are always listening
item.description: The trouble with virtual assistants is that they’re just so darn needy. Specifically, they need to be told exactly what to do. They sit around doing nothing until explicitly directed. They don’t take any initiative. They’re, for lack of a better word, lazy.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Las empresas europeas están menos dispuestas a invertir en TI que las estadounidenses
item.description: Las grandes empresas estadounidenses con ventas anuales de más de 1.000 millones de dólares invierten el 1,45 % de sus ventas en tecnología y TI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data
item.description: A Treasury official says DOGE team members have “read-only” access to the data from its payment systems as part of an “operational efficiency assessment." The post DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Warren finds common ground with Trump over debanking concerns
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) seems to have found common ground with President Trump and Republican lawmakers over “debanking” concerns, urging the president Tuesday to take action to prevent Americans from being improperly locked out of the financial system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google Revises AI Principles, Opens Door to AI-Powered Weapons and Surveillance
item.description: Google revises its AI principles, removing prohibitions on engaging in activities like creating AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Thinking of moving off VMware? Gartner tallies cost of large-scale VMware migration
item.description: Broadcom’s $61 billion acquisition of VMware caused quite a stir in the technology industry and continues to prompt VMware customers to consider alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
